questions.q: Which of the following properties of amines is due to the presence of lone pair of electrons on nitrogen atom?
Give one example of a primary amine. Write its chemical formula and structure.
Explain the reason for lower boiling point of tertiary amines as compared to secondary and primary amines.
questions.answer: Due to the presence of larger size and weaker intermolecular forces of hydrogen bonding, the boiling point is lower.

questions.q: An amine is obtained by the reaction of ethyl chloride with ammonia. Is the amine primary, secondary or tertiary? Explain.
Write the chemical reaction for the preparation of aniline from nitrobenzene.
Fill in the blank: Amines having general formula RNH₂, R₂NH and R₃N are called ________ amines.
Give two differences between primary and secondary amines.
questions.answer: 1. Primary amines have higher boiling points than secondary amines due to the presence of strong intermolecular forces of hydrogen bonding. 2. Primary amines are more basic than secondary amines.

questions.q: Explain why aniline is not obtained by the direct reaction of benzene with ammonia.
questions.answer: Aniline is not obtained by the direct reaction of benzene with ammonia because the reaction is not feasible due to the absence of an acidic hydrogen in benzene.

questions.q: Write the chemical reaction for the preparation of methylamine from sodium nitroprusside.
questions.answer: Sodium nitroprusside + H₂O + NH₃ → Methylamine + NaNO₂ + NaOH

questions.q: True or False: Aromatic amines are more basic than aliphatic amines.
